cases.title: Concept gap in fraction subtraction
cases.objective: Show that repeated same-topic misses with slower response patterns support a bounded concept-gap hypothesis.
observed: Two incorrect answers on the same topic
cases.teacher_review_framing: Use this as a likely prerequisite gap worth checking before the next assessment, not as a proof of the student's exact misconception.
cases.unsafe_overclaim: The system has proven the exact internal misunderstanding without teacher review.

cases.title: Shared misconception becomes a small-group move
cases.objective: Show that the evidence loop can move from shared signals to a concrete teacher move instead of stopping at analysis.
observed: Three students share the same topic-level misconception
All three required repeated hints before reaching an answer
The diagnosis layer grouped them under one recommendation
cases.teacher_review_framing: The system is proposing one bounded classroom move: pull the students into a remediation mini-group around the same misconception.
cases.unsafe_overclaim: This grouping guarantees the best intervention outcome or proves that no other grouping would work.

cases.title: Abstain when the evidence layer is too thin
cases.objective: Show that the system withholds diagnosis when one strong-correct signal is not enough to justify intervention.

inferred.abstain_reason: evidence is too weak or too mixed for a confident diagnosis

cases.teacher_review_framing: This is a trust feature: the system avoids fabricating intervention when the observation layer does not justify one.
cases.unsafe_overclaim: No diagnosis means the student fully mastered the topic or needs no future review at all.

cases.title: Teacher converts a recommendation into a bounded remediation step
cases.objective: Show that the product now supports the path from recommendation to structured teacher action and intervention assignment.
observed: A student repeatedly misses one prerequisite concept
The dashboard recommends reteaching that prerequisite
The teacher creates a structured action and later turns it into an intervention assignment
cases.teacher_review_framing: The system helps the teacher operationalize the insight, but the teacher remains responsible for confirming and executing the intervention.
cases.unsafe_overclaim: Creating a teacher action proves the intervention was effective or completed successfully.

cases.title: Bounded reason trace supports trust without chain-of-thought exposure
cases.objective: Show that the system exposes provenance and teacher-review framing while keeping rationale bounded and audit-friendly.
observed: The insight payload includes observed evidence facts
The dashboard shows diagnosis policy and teacher-review-required framing
The /agents authoring surface shows runtime policy audit slices and sources
cases.teacher_review_framing: These traces are bounded trust surfaces for review and debugging, not unrestricted model reasoning dumps.
cases.unsafe_overclaim: The product now exposes full internal chain-of-thought or proves universal explainability across all paths.
